us-politics.headline: Two US Warplanes Down Over Iran, Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body: Two US warplanes were shot down during operations near Iran, with search efforts underway for at least one missing crew member. The incident has sharpened debate in Washington over the scope and direction of US military engagement. Rep. Nancy Mace voiced concern about escalation, questioning whether Congress has been adequately consulted on ground troop deployments. NPR analysis notes Trump's wartime rhetoric departs sharply from the measured language of past presidents, a contrast supporters say reflects resolve and critics call reckless. A Democratic congressman separately condemned prediction market Polymarket for allowing bets on the fate of the downed pilot, calling it a moral failure. Public sentiment is uneasy - roughly half of Americans surveyed expect Middle East stability to worsen.

sources.title: Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
Mace ‘concerned about escalation’ in Iran war
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters
About half expect worsening stability in Middle East: Survey

us-politics.headline: Hegseth Ousts Army Chief; Republicans Push Back Hard
us-politics.body: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed the Army chief of staff, a move that drew an unusual wave of criticism from within the Republican Party. Multiple GOP lawmakers praised the ousted general publicly, signaling rare friction between congressional Republicans and the Pentagon leadership. The episode adds to a broader pattern of tension inside the military establishment under Hegseth. Separately, FBI officials notified Congress of a major data breach affecting a surveillance system, compounding concerns about institutional stability across federal agencies. Todd Blanche, now running the Justice Department day-to-day, faces mounting pressure to manage Trump's expectations while maintaining prosecutorial credibility, according to reporting from the Washington Post.

sources.title: GOP praise pours in for Army chief of staff ousted by Hegseth
FBI labels data breach ‘major incident,’ notifies Congress
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Targets TSA, Alcatraz, and DC Overhaul
us-politics.body: Trump's latest budget proposals span a wide range. The administration wants to cut TSA funding and force privatisation at smaller airports, raising questions about security wait times at regional hubs. A separate $10 billion fund would finance construction and beautification projects across Washington DC. The budget also requests $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a high-security federal prison. Critics see the proposals as a mix of ideological priorities and spectacle. Supporters argue the TSA cuts push efficiency and that investment in the capital reflects national ambition. VP Vance was named a fraud czar tasked with targeting what the White House describes as taxpayer theft in Democratic-led states, a framing those states reject.

sources.title: Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects
Trump seeks $152M to rebuild Alcatraz as ‘state-of-the-art secure prison’
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft

us-politics.headline: Voting Rights, Epstein Files, and Colorado ICE Dispute Simmer
us-politics.body: Democratic-led states filed suit against Trump's executive order on mail-in voting, arguing it oversteps federal authority over election administration. The order, signed days ago, drew immediate legal challenge. In Colorado, lawyers using the state court e-filing system report being required to certify they will not assist ICE - a requirement critics say is legally dubious and politically motivated. On the Epstein front, Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche said there is no evidence the late financier was a foreign spy, but Rep. Nancy Mace is pressing for Attorney General Pam Bondi to testify publicly on the files. Senate Democrats are separately demanding answers from the Treasury Department over the decision to add Trump's name to US currency.

sources.title: Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order
Trump issues order attempting to change rules for mail-in voting
Colorado lawyers say court e-file system now makes them certify they won’t assist ICE
Blanche: No evidence in Epstein files he was a spy
Mace ‘not backing away’ from Bondi testifying on Epstein
Senate Democrats press Treasury Department on decision to add Trump name to currency

au-politics.headline: Tax Policy Divides Parties as Election Battle Sharpens
au-politics.body:
Both Labor and the Coalition are under pressure to match voter expectations on tax, with debate intensifying over whether mining companies should pay more. Even Liberal MP Andrew Hastie has signalled openness to higher levies on miners, complicating the Coalition's position ahead of the federal election.

Albanese and shadow treasurer Angus Taylor are each trying to hold credible economic ground without alienating key constituencies. The tension reflects a broader contest over cost-of-living relief, with voters demanding action on prices while both parties resist structural tax changes that could spook business.

sources.title: Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations

au-politics.headline: One Nation Leaves SA Liberals Scrambling for Opposition Status
au-politics.body:
South Australia's Liberal Party is fighting to retain its formal opposition status after One Nation's strong showing reshaped the state parliament's crossbench. The situation has few modern precedents, leaving the Liberals in an awkward position where their standing in the chamber is no longer guaranteed.

The result highlights the growing pressure on traditional conservative parties from minor-party challengers on the right. For One Nation, the outcome marks a significant step in its push for parliamentary relevance beyond Queensland.

sources.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

au-politics.headline: Iran War Ripples Hit Asia as Hormuz Sailors Stranded
au-politics.body:
The conflict involving Iran is sending economic shockwaves well beyond the Middle East. The Philippines is sliding toward an energy crisis as gas supplies tighten, while roughly 20,000 sailors remain stranded aboard supertankers in the Gulf with no clear exit as the Strait of Hormuz stays effectively closed to normal traffic.

Iran's downing of a US jet has added a sharp new dimension to the crisis. Analysts say the move will shock American public opinion in ways the White House has not fully prepared for, raising the stakes for US decision-making in a conflict that is already disrupting global shipping and fuel markets.

sources.title: Iran's downing of a US jet will shock Americans in three ways
The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf
How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

au-politics.headline: EV Novated Leases Surge as Fuel Costs Bite Travellers
au-politics.body:
More Australians are using novated leases to access electric vehicles under a tax arrangement that significantly cuts the effective purchase price. The scheme, which allows pre-tax salary to cover vehicle costs, has grown sharply as petrol prices remain high and EV options expand.

Long-term caravanners are feeling the fuel squeeze hardest, with some describing the situation as a second lockdown as rising costs force them to cut trips short or stay put. The contrast between those able to switch to EVs and those locked into petrol-dependent lifestyles is becoming a visible fault line in how Australians experience the energy transition.

sources.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know
Another 'lockdown': How long-term caravanners are dealing with the fuel crisis

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Jets Downed, Hormuz Reopens
world.body:
Iran says it shot down two US fighter jets, with search operations underway for at least one pilot. Survivors of a US-Israeli strike on an Iranian bridge described the attack, while the WHO reported more than 20 strikes on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1. A factory in Israel sustained damage from Iranian missile debris.

Despite the fighting, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumed, with Omani, French, and Japanese vessels transiting the waterway - the first passage since hostilities began. The reopening raises urgent questions about whether the strait can remain navigable, or whether force will be needed to keep it open. An intercepted attack on a UAE gas facility killed at least one person, signaling the conflict's regional reach.

sources.title: Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack
French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran
Omani, French and Japanese vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz
Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris
Iran says it downed two US jets as search for one pilot continues

world.headline: Trump Requests $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget, Slashing Domestic Spending
world.body:
President Trump has asked Congress to approve a record $1.5 trillion defence budget, the largest military spending request in US history. The proposal pairs massive Pentagon investment with deep cuts to domestic programmes, sharpening a political battle with Democrats already suing the administration over mail-in ballot restrictions.

A federal judge upheld the dismissal of subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, handing the White House a setback in its pressure campaign on the central bank. US unemployment fell despite the economic turbulence of the Iran war, offering the administration a rare piece of positive economic news.

sources.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war
Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners Under US Pressure
world.body:
Cuba began releasing more than 2,000 prisoners following sustained pressure from Washington. The move marks a notable concession from Havana, though analysts caution it falls short of the broader political reforms the US has demanded. The releases come as the Cuban government faces deepening economic strain.

Elsewhere, a drone strike on a hospital in Sudan killed 10 people, according to medical charity MSF. Christians in Lebanon observed Good Friday under Israeli bombardment, while monks held Easter prayers in a largely deserted Old City of Jerusalem.

sources.title: Christians in Lebanon observe Good Friday under Israeli attacks
Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure
Monks hold Easter prayers in deserted Old City of Jerusalem
Drone strike on Sudan hospital kills 10, medical charity MSF says

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sends Back Stunning Earth Images
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew captured striking photographs of Earth from deep space, offering some of the most vivid imagery of the planet since the Apollo era. The images, released publicly by NASA, show the Earth in sharp detail against the blackness of space and have drawn widespread attention.

The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's push to return humans to the Moon, with the Artemis II crew conducting the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The photographs serve as both a scientific record and a rare moment of shared wonder amid a turbulent global news cycle.

sources.title: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission

ai.headline: OpenAI Shuffles Executives as AGI Lead Steps Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reshuffling its senior ranks, with COO Brad Lightcap moving into a new 'special projects' role and CEO applications chief Fidji Simo taking medical leave. The company's AGI research lead is also stepping away, leaving a notable gap at the top of its most consequential technical division.

The simultaneous departures suggest internal strain at a company navigating rapid commercial expansion alongside existential research goals. OpenAI has not named interim replacements for the absent executives, and the timing - amid fierce competition from Anthropic and Google - raises questions about leadership continuity.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: Anthropic Buys Biotech, Launches PAC, Eyes Washington
ai.body:
Anthropic is moving fast on two fronts. The company reportedly acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, signaling a serious push into AI-driven biology and drug discovery. It also launched a political action committee, formalizing its lobbying ambitions in Washington.

The dual moves paint a picture of a company that sees its future as both a scientific platform and a policy player. Rivals like OpenAI have already cultivated White House ties. Anthropic appears determined to catch up, spending heavily to shape both the science and the rules of the road.

sources.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: AI's Power Hunger Collides With Politics and Public Backlash
ai.body:
The infrastructure underpinning the AI boom is running into serious headwinds. Trump's tariffs are driving up construction costs for the data centers his administration publicly champions, undermining the buildout before it scales. Meanwhile, AI companies are turning to large natural gas plants to meet power demand, drawing environmental scrutiny.

Public sentiment is souring too. A new survey finds people would sooner accept an Amazon warehouse near their home than a data center - citing noise, heat, and water use. The gap between political ambition and on-the-ground reality is widening fast.

sources.title: Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center

ai.headline: Meta Data Breach and Musk's IPO Squeeze Rattle Tech World
ai.body:
Meta has paused its relationship with AI hiring platform Mercor after a data breach exposed sensitive information about the company's AI projects. The incident highlights how third-party contractors have become a soft underbelly for major AI firms handling proprietary research.

Separately, Elon Musk is reportedly pressuring banks working on a SpaceX IPO to purchase Grok subscriptions as a condition of the business relationship. The move blurs the line between dealmaking and product promotion in ways that are drawing sharp criticism. Both stories point to a tech industry where leverage - and its abuse - is increasingly on display.

sources.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions

btc.headline: Circle Faces Heat Over $285M Hack and Compliance Failures
btc.body:
Circle is under serious pressure after the $285 million Drift protocol hack exposed the stablecoin issuer's reluctance to freeze stolen USDC. Critics argue Circle's inaction undermines the core value proposition of a regulated, freezable stablecoin.

On-chain investigator ZachXBT piled on, accusing Circle of over $420 million in compliance failures since 2022. The back-to-back allegations paint a damaging picture of a company preparing for a public listing while struggling to demonstrate basic financial crime controls.

The timing is brutal. Circle's credibility as a compliant, institutional-grade issuer is central to its market position, and these incidents give regulators and competitors fresh ammunition.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022

btc.headline: Schwab Enters Spot Crypto Trading as Bitcoin Dips Below $70K
btc.body:
Charles Schwab confirmed plans to launch direct spot Bitcoin and Ether trading in the first half of 2026, a major signal that traditional brokerage giants are done sitting out the crypto market. The move follows similar steps by competitors and brings tens of millions of retail accounts closer to direct crypto exposure.

The announcement lands as Bitcoin trades flat and briefly dipped below $70,000, with corporate treasury holders showing signs of diverging strategy. Some firms are holding firm while others are reassessing their BTC positions under macro pressure.

Altcoins edged higher on the day, with the broader CoinDesk 20 index outperforming Bitcoin. XRP climbed to $1.33 but failed to break out, while Cathie Wood reiterated a $34,000 Bitcoin floor target, arguing the era of 85% drawdowns is over.

sources.title: Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026
What next as XRP rises to $1.33 but fails to break out
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Hits 70,000 ETH Staking Target
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed its staking push, deploying another $93 million in ETH to reach its self-imposed 70,000 ETH target. The move is a deliberate effort to demonstrate institutional confidence in Ethereum's proof-of-stake network and generate yield on otherwise idle treasury assets.

Separately, Offchain Labs argued that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive fee pricing to scale to billions of users, pointing to structural inefficiencies that could limit adoption. The two developments together reflect an Ethereum ecosystem actively working to shore up both its economic model and its infrastructure ahead of the next growth cycle.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation nearly reaches 70,000 staked ETH goal
Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Banking Opposition as Crypto Regulation Heats Up
btc.body:
US community banks formally opposed the OCC's move to grant Coinbase a trust charter, arguing it gives the crypto exchange an unfair regulatory advantage without the full obligations traditional banks carry. The pushback reflects growing tension between legacy financial institutions and crypto firms gaining ground in regulated markets.

Meanwhile, Cambodia proposed harsh prison sentences for crypto scammers, and Malta clashed with the EU's ESMA over centralized oversight of crypto regulation. Across multiple jurisdictions, the rules governing crypto are tightening fast - and the fights over who writes them are just beginning.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Cambodian lawmakers propose severe prison time for crypto scammers
Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Farmer's Carry Ladder
exercise.description: Walk 20 metres carrying heavy weights at your sides, rest 30 seconds, then increase load each round — brutally simple grip, core, and trap builder that most people never try

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memory, and selfhood
learn.why: On a Friday it hits differently: the person who started this week is measurably not the same one finishing it, and spending an hour tracing how Locke, Parfit, and modern neuroscience each answer that will quietly rewire how you think about change and continuity
